title: Trademark Registration

hero.headline: Register Your Trademark with Expert Legal Guidance
hero.subtext: Protect your brand name, logo, and identity with end-to-end trademark filing from Professionals Law.

mainHeading: Trademark Registration Online – Apply for Brand & Logo Trademark

introduction.heading: Introduction and Its Compliance
body: A Trademark can be a single logo, word, symbol or a combination that sets apart your products and services from those of another in the same market system. The Registration of Trademark provides exclusive ownership and legal rights under the Trade Marks Act, 1999. It is an essential part of brand identity, as it can help businesses establish themselves and stand out from the competition. Professionals Law offers seamless trademark services across the nation. From trademark search and classification to filing, including objection and opposition handling, Professionals Law makes sure that your brand is protected and fully compliant under the law, with all the statutory framework that is needed.

whyNeeded.heading: Why It Is Needed
body: Protecting your brand from being imitated and used unauthorizedly is crucial and that is where trademark registration comes into picture. It grants its owner a monopoly in use of the mark with respect to a particular product or service. Without having it registered, your competitors could take advantage of your name and you will have a hard time enforcing your rights. Professionals Law ensures that your brand’s image and identity is protected across all the legal fronts.

body.title: Exclusive Rights for Ownership
body.description: Once you register your trademark, you own your brand name or logo and become the only party that can use it; no other company or person can lawfully use your brand name or logo.

body.title: Legal Shield
body.description: Trademarks that are registered have legal backing. We at Professionals Law assist you in filing suit for any kind of violation or replication.

body.title: Brand Value & Name
body.description: A trade mark is associated with a business and helps a business with recognition and goodwill. As it grows it becomes an inimitable company asset.

body.title: Nation-Wide Protection
body.description: Once a trademark has been registered with Professionals Law, it enjoys protection all over India, irrespective of the locality to which your business operates.

body.title: Essential for Business Growth
body.description: Trademark is an essential asset when seeking out new investors, franchising, licensing and while expanding your business overseas. Professionals Law also assists with international trademark registration, if required.

body.title: Credibility
body.description: A registered trademark gives your brand credibility in the eyes of consumers, investors and partners.

criteria: An individual, company, partnership, LLP or NGO
The mark must be used or must have a bona fide intention to be used in future.
The sign has to be distinctive and unique.
The proposed trademark must not infringe on one that is already registered.

documents: Id and address proof of the applicant (Aadhaar, PAN, Passport, etc.)
Business registration documents (GST, Certificate of Incorporation, etc.)
Proof of trademark usage (if already in use)
MSME/Startup certificate (for government fee concessions, if applicable)

body.description: Professionals Law will search your trademark in depth to be sure it is unique and is not already registered.
A new trademark application is filed within the Trademark Registry electronically in Form TM-A).
The Trademark Office examines your application and reports an examination result within 1 to 2 months.
In case of any objections, Professionals Law prepares a robust reply supported with legal justifications.
After acceptance, the trademark is published in the Trademark Journal for any public opposition.
If there is any opposition raised, Professionals Law will handle the opposition matters, including legal replies and hearings.
If no objection is made or any opposition is filed or if they are resolved, the trademark will be registered and a certificate will be issued.

body.title: Infringement
body.description: Any unauthorized use of a trademark may result in civil and criminal proceedings and may cost monetary damages and/or imprisonment.

body.title: False Representation
body.description: Providing false, inaccurate or misleading information, documents or misrepresentation may subject to application denial and legal consequences.

body.title: Non-Usage
body.description: If a trademark is not used for 5 consecutive years post-registration, it might be removed from the registry.

body.title: Trademark Search & Filing
body.description: 1 to 2 working days

body.title: Examination Report
body.description: 30 to 60 days

body.title: Publication in Journal
body.description: After acceptance, around 4 to 6 months

body.title: Opposition Period
body.description: 4 months from the date of publication in journal

body.title: Registration Certificate
body.description: 6 to 18 months (depending upon any objections or opposition)

body.question: Who can file a trademark in India?
body.answer: Anybody - individual, company, LLP, NGO, irrespective of the size or turnover is eligible to apply.

body.question: Can I register a trademark before starting the business?
body.answer: Yes, you can register a trademark on a ‘proposed to be used’ basis before even starting your business.

body.question: What is the validity period of trademark?
body.answer: The trademark registration remains valid for a period of 10 years and it can be renewed indefinitely.

body.question: Is logo registration different from name registration?
body.answer: Yes. You can register either or both a wordmark (name) and/or a device mark (logo). Professionals Law helps you in securing both of them.

body.question: What if my trademark/brand is similar to someone else?
body.answer: Professionals Law makes thorough search before applying. In case there is any issue, our legal experts will take care of your application.

body.question: Is it possible for a foreign company to register a trademark in India?
body.answer: Absolutely yes, even a foreign companies can apply under Indian trademark law.

body.question: Can I use the ™ symbol even if it is not registered?
body.answer: Yes. Once you apply, you can start using ™ without any problem. Use ® only after registration.

body.question: What if someone else copies my trademark?
body.answer: Trademark laws give you legal rights to take action against those who infringe or misuse your trademark. Professionals Law can assist you in sending cease & desist notices or file lawsuit against them.

body.question: Is online trademark registration reliable?
body.answer: Yes, Professionals Law uses secure and government-approved portals for all online filing of applications, submissions, ensuring transparency and reliability.
